artifacts.title: Beveiligingsadvies NCSC-2026-0076
artifacts.summary: High priority security advisory on Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center vulnerabilities
artifacts.objective: To inform about critical vulnerabilities in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center and urge immediate patching.
artifacts.what_changed: Two critical vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-20079 and CVE-2026-20131) allowing authentication bypass and remote code execution with root privileges were disclosed, impacting Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center products.
artifacts.why_it_matters: These vulnerabilities pose a high risk of unauthorized root access and code execution, potentially compromising network security and operations.
artifacts.risk_impact: Failure to patch promptly may lead to exploitation resulting in full device compromise, operational disruption, and potential data breaches.
key_requirements: Install Cisco updates to remediate the vulnerabilities immediately.
If using Cisco Security Cloud Control Firewall Management (SaaS), no user action is required as updates are automatic.
Avoid exposing management interfaces directly to the public internet to reduce attack surface.

actionables.other_change: Monitor for public proof-of-concept exploits and increased attack attempts.
actionables.process_change: Implement patch management process to apply Cisco updates immediately.
actionables.tech_data_change: Update Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center software to latest patched versions.

penalties_consequences: Potential for severe security breaches including unauthorized root access.
Operational disruption and reputational damage due to exploitation.
Legal liability risks under Dutch law for failure to maintain adequate cybersecurity.

other_notes: Organizations using Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center and Cisco Security Cloud Control Firewall Management

Correction of identified deficiencies within 12 months after report submission

Maintain adequate risk management systems as required by section 912A of the Corporations Act.
Implement robust processes for approving financial products for recommendation.
Respond appropriately to use of lead generators and conflicts of interest.
Prevent negative consent practices and ensure express client consent for investments.
Provide adequate responses to client complaints and compliance issues.

actionables.other_change: Respond to ASIC proceedings and implement corrective actions as ordered by the court.
actionables.policy_change: Revise product approval and compliance oversight policies.
actionables.status_change: Potential restriction or barring from carrying on financial services business.
actionables.process_change: Enhance risk management and client consent processes; improve complaint handling.
actionables.reporting_change: Increase transparency and reporting on product approvals and compliance issues.
actionables.tech_data_change: Update systems to track approved product lists and client consent records accurately.

penalties_consequences: Civil penalties under sections 961L and 912A(5A) of the Corporations Act.
Court orders restraining Interprac from carrying on financial services business.
Potential reputational damage and loss of client trust.
Costs payable to ASIC.

artifacts.title: Feedback Statement - Anti-Greenwashing Guidance and changes to Code of Corporate Governance
artifacts.objective: To introduce a new Guidance Note on Anti-Greenwashing and update the Finance Sector Code of Corporate Governance to include environmental sustainability risks, reinforcing integrity and transparency in sustainability-related communications and governance.
artifacts.what_changed: Introduction of an Anti-Greenwashing Guidance Note applicable to all licensed persons, clarifying expectations to avoid misleading environmental claims; amendments to the Finance Sector Code of Corporate Governance requiring boards to consider relevant environmental sustainability risks including climate change and biodiversity risks.
artifacts.why_it_matters: The update addresses growing regulatory and stakeholder focus on sustainability and greenwashing, ensuring licensees act with integrity and manage environmental risks appropriately, thereby protecting the reputation of the Bailiwick as a responsible financial centre.
artifacts.risk_impact: The update reinforces existing integrity and risk management duties, with minimal additional burden on well-run firms, but increases focus on environmental risks which may affect strategic and reputational risk profiles.
key_requirements: Licensees must ensure communications about environmental sustainability characteristics are consistent, fair, clear, and not misleading.
Boards must consider the impact of relevant environmental sustainability risks, including climate change and biodiversity risks, on business strategy and risk profile.
Boards should provide suitable oversight of risk management and maintain sound systems of risk measurement and control, including environmental risks.
Designated Administrators not responsible for preparing prospectuses are not expected to have new obligations under the Guidance Note.
Periodic reconsideration of environmental sustainability risks is required as risks and business models evolve.

actionables.other_change: Boards to periodically reassess environmental sustainability risks in line with evolving business models and external risk landscape.
actionables.policy_change: Update internal policies to incorporate Anti-Greenwashing Guidance and environmental sustainability risk considerations in governance.
actionables.process_change: Implement processes to review and ensure accuracy and fairness of sustainability-related communications; integrate environmental risk assessment into board risk oversight.
actionables.training_change: Train relevant staff on Anti-Greenwashing expectations and environmental risk considerations in governance and communications.
actionables.reporting_change: Where appropriate, make timely climate change related disclosures as judged by the board.

penalties_consequences: Material breaches of the Minimum Criteria for Licensing, including Anti-Greenwashing provisions, are treated very seriously by the Commission, potentially leading to regulatory sanctions.
Reputational damage for firms engaging in greenwashing or failing to manage environmental risks appropriately.

artifacts.title: MEMORANDUM
artifacts.summary: Legal analysis on FHLBank authority to offer caps, collars, and floors
artifacts.objective: Clarify the authority of Federal Home Loan Banks (FHLBanks) under the Financial Management Policy (FMP) regarding offering caps, collars, and floors to members.
artifacts.what_changed: The FMP does not authorize FHLBanks to offer caps, collars, and floors to members for asset/liability management; only swaps are authorized for this purpose. The Finance Board acting without a quorum cannot amend the FMP to grant such authority.
artifacts.why_it_matters: This clarification limits the types of derivative instruments FHLBanks can offer members, impacting their asset/liability management strategies and ensuring compliance with existing policy and statutory authority.
artifacts.risk_impact: Restricts FHLBanks' ability to offer certain option-based derivatives to members, potentially limiting risk management tools available to members and requiring strict adherence to existing policy to avoid unauthorized transactions.
key_requirements: FHLBanks may engage in cap, collar, and floor transactions only for their own accounts, not to facilitate members' asset/liability management.
FHLBanks may act as intermediaries in swap transactions for members but not for caps, collars, and floors.
Finance Board without a quorum cannot amend the FMP to expand FHLBanks' authority to offer these instruments to members.

artifacts.objective: To notify Varigard, LLC of violations regarding unapproved new drug products related to COVID-19 and to require immediate corrective action.
artifacts.what_changed: FDA identified Varigard's alcohol-based topical antiseptic products as unapproved new drugs and misbranded due to unauthorized COVID-19 claims and labeling violations.
artifacts.why_it_matters: Unapproved and misleading claims about COVID-19 treatment pose public health risks and violate federal law, potentially prolonging the pandemic and increasing morbidity and mortality.

actionables.reporting_change: Submit corrective action report to FDA within 48 hours.
actionables.tech_data_change: Update website and digital content to remove unauthorized claims and FDA logos.

Potential financial penalties and loss of market access.

artifacts.title: FDIC Board of Directors Issues an Interim Final Rule to Amend the Collection of the Special Assessment
artifacts.summary: FDIC issues interim final rule adjusting special assessment collection rates
artifacts.objective: To amend the collection of the special assessment to recover losses to the Deposit Insurance Fund arising from the systemic risk exception related to Silicon Valley Bank and Signature Bank failures.
artifacts.what_changed: The interim final rule reduces the special assessment rate for the eighth collection quarter from 3.36 basis points to 2.97 basis points and requires offsets to regular quarterly deposit insurance assessments if collections exceed losses after litigation resolution or receivership termination.
artifacts.why_it_matters: This update ensures accurate recovery of losses without overcollecting or undercollecting, minimizing financial impact on banks and maintaining regulatory compliance.
artifacts.risk_impact: Reduces risk of overcollection and financial strain on banks; introduces potential adjustments based on litigation outcomes, impacting cash flow and assessment liabilities.
key_requirements: Collect special assessment at reduced rate of 2.97 basis points for eighth quarter.
Provide offsets to regular deposit insurance assessments if aggregate special assessment collections exceed losses after litigation resolution or receivership termination.
Implement a one-time final shortfall special assessment if losses exceed amounts collected at receivership termination.
Remove extended assessment period provisions as they are no longer necessary.

actionables.other_change: Communicate changes to impacted entities and stakeholders; prepare for potential one-time final shortfall assessment.
actionables.policy_change: Adopt interim final rule amendments to special assessment collection procedures.
actionables.process_change: Adjust special assessment collection rate for eighth quarter; implement offset procedures post-litigation and receivership termination.
actionables.reporting_change: Monitor and report special assessment collections and offsets accurately in regulatory filings.
actionables.tech_data_change: Update systems to calculate reduced special assessment rate and apply offsets to regular deposit insurance assessments as required.

penalties_consequences: Non-compliance may result in financial penalties and reputational damage due to failure to meet statutory loss recovery requirements.
Overcollection or undercollection could lead to regulatory scrutiny and corrective actions.
